pi_items.source: Web search: CDC Behavioral Risk Factor Surveillance System is a real national health survey system. BRFSS is the official acronym used by CDC for this survey.
Code analysis: LimeSurvey format codes documented in export_result.sh line 42: G=Group-by-Group, S=Question-by-Question, A=All-in-one. Task description requires 'Format: Group by group'.
Code analysis: Task description (task.json:19) states 'Allow backward navigation: Off (Interviewers must not go back)'. LimeSurvey stores this as 'N' in lime_surveys table (export_result.sh:50).
Code analysis: Task description (task.json:18) states 'Show progress bar: On'. LimeSurvey stores this as 'Y' in lime_surveys table (export_result.sh:51).
Code analysis: Task description (task.json:20) states 'Date stamp: On'. LimeSurvey stores this as 'Y' in lime_surveys table (export_result.sh:52).
Code analysis: Task description (task.json:8-9) requires 'Create at least 3 question groups representing BRFSS core sections'. Verified in verifier.py:122 and export_result.sh:71.

Code analysis: Task description (task.json:10-11) requires 'Add at least 5 real BRFSS-style questions'. Verified in verifier.py:130 and export_result.sh:74. Count excludes subquestions (parent_qid=0).

Web search + Code analysis: BRFSS interviews mention state health departments, random phone selection, and voluntary participation. Task description (task.json:13-15) requires welcome message include introduction from 'State Department of Health', number chosen 'randomly', and survey is 'voluntary'. Verified in verifier.py:102-107.
Code analysis: Task description (task.json:16-17) requires end message include 'thank you' and mention 'health' or 'community'. Verified in verifier.py:113-118.

privileged_info_summary: BRFSS (Behavioral Risk Factor Surveillance System) is a real, CDC-operated national health surveillance system established in 1984. It is the world's largest continuously conducted health survey, completing over 400,000 adult interviews annually across all 50 states and territories. The survey covers core sections including Health Status, Health Care Access, and Health Behaviors, with standard questions addressing general health rating, health insurance coverage, and physical activity/exercise. BRFSS interviews are conducted by phone from state health departments, using randomly selected phone numbers, and participation is completely voluntary. This task requires agents to create a LimeSurvey configuration implementing BRFSS-style survey methodology with verified settings: Group-by-Group format, disabled backward navigation (critical for CATI protocol), enabled progress bar and date stamping, at least 3 question groups, and at least 5 BRFSS-style questions with appropriate interviewer scripts containing keywords 'health', 'department', 'random', and 'voluntary' in welcome text, plus 'thank' and 'health' in closing text.
